notes_md:
**mới mẻ** = new in a fresh, exciting, or different way. It’s not just “new,” but something that feels refreshing or unfamiliar in a good way.

It’s an adjective. It usually describes experiences, ideas, feelings, perspectives, environments.  

Compared to **mới**, *mới mẻ* focuses more on the feeling of freshness, not just something recently created.  

Opposite ideas: **quen thuộc** (familiar), **cũ kỹ** (old-fashioned, worn out).

translation.en: This dish is very new and appealing.

content.sentence: Món ăn này rất ___ và hấp dẫn.
Cô ấy luôn tìm kiếm điều ___ trong cuộc sống.

translation.en: She always seeks something new in life.

notes:
mới mẻ = new in a fresh, exciting, or different way. It’s not just “new,” but something that feels refreshing or unfamiliar in a good way.

It’s an adjective. It usually describes experiences, ideas, feelings, perspectives, environments.  

Compared to mới, mới mẻ focuses more on the feeling of freshness, not just something recently created.  

Opposite ideas: quen thuộc (familiar), cũ kỹ (old-fashioned, worn out).

example.es: La sensación fresca al comenzar un nuevo trabajo es maravillosa.
example.en: The fresh feeling when starting a new job is wonderful.
example.vi: Cảm giác mới mẻ khi bắt đầu một công việc mới thật tuyệt.

definitions.vi: Mới và tươi mới; sáng tạo.

exampleSentences: Anh muốn thử một điều gì đó mới mẻ vào cuối tuần này.
Công việc mới mẻ khiến chị cảm thấy hào hứng mỗi ngày.
Cuốn sách này mang đến cho em nhiều ý tưởng mới mẻ.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I want to try something new this weekend.
The new job makes me feel excited every day.
This book gives me many new ideas.
